case_rows.detail: the committed article-shaped Sudoku-9x9 test case remains the explicit hard-Sudoku suite stand-in already referenced by TAS-175 and the Sudoku article reproducer
the named Arto Inkala puzzle is added as the canonical public hard-Sudoku benchmark case and must stay exact on HullCache under the article's under-three-minute ceiling

claim_boundary: this manifest defines only the TAS-181 hard-Sudoku suite: one committed stand-in plus the named Arto Inkala case, both bound to the canonical Sudoku article source and a 180 second runtime ceiling. It does not itself claim the later unified demo-and-benchmark equivalence gate or final article-equivalence green status.
